insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers can use this new feature to build more customized and channel-specific experiences for merchants. They will need to update their Storefront API queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change does not introduce any breaking changes, but rather provides an additional feature for developers to utilize. Developers should review their existing Storefront API queries and consider where the @inContext directive with channelId can be applied to improve their app's functionality.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more customized and channel-specific experiences, potentially leading to increased sales and customer satisfaction. This update enables developers to build more tailored solutions for merchants, which can enhance the overall merchant experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update matters because it provides developers with more flexibility and control over the data they fetch from the Storefront API, allowing them to build more customized and effective solutions for merchants. By leveraging the @inContext directive with channelId, developers can create more targeted and relevant experiences for merchants and their customers.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
The Shopify CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: The App Store requirements have been updated to include a unique name for each app, reducing merchant confusion over identical and similar app names.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports the channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields, providing more information about exchange line items.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: The POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
The Shopify CLI version 4.5.1 bumps cli-hydrogen to 13.0.1, fixing issues and improving the overall development experience.
The Shopify CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation, making it easier to manage stores.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen version 13.0.1 fixes issues with command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during deployment.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: The @shopify/create-hydrogen version 5.0.38 adds an explicit app alias to new Hydrogen projects and improves JavaScript project configuration.

recap.headline: Shopify updates for GraphQL Admin API, Hydrogen, and Storefront API

themes.title: New Features and Updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId`. Additionally, POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, and new fields have been added to `ExchangeLineItem`.

themes.title: Hydrogen Updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released, with features such as non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and explicit `~ app` alias to new Hydrogen projects. The `skeleton` package has also been updated to version 2026.4.5.

themes.title: Security and App Requirements
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps must use a unique name to reduce merchant confusion. The updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for the app.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use a unique name
Implement mTLS certificate for card deposit endpoint
